content.problemStatement:
Ada has two matrices AAA and BBB, each with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN).

Ada may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero): choose an integer iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), then swap row iii and column iii in matrix AAA. (Formally, for each valid jjj, she swaps Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​ with Aj,iA_{j, i}Aj,i​.)

Is it possible to transform matrix AAA into matrix BBB?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the size of the matrices. Then, N lines follow with N space-separated integers each, representing matrix A. After that, another N lines follow with N space-separated integers each, representing matrix B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'Yes' if it is possible to transform matrix A into matrix B by swapping rows with columns, and 'No' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: We can swap the second row with the second column and in the resulting matrix, swap the first row with the first column.
Explanation: We can swap the first row with the first column.
Explanation: It is not possible to transform the first matrix into the second matrix by swapping rows with columns.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy approach. We start by comparing the first row of matrix A with the first column of matrix B. If they are equal, we can swap the first row with the first column in matrix A. We repeat this process for all rows and columns. If at any point we find a mismatch, we return 'No'. If we successfully transform matrix A into matrix B, we return 'Yes'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the size of the matrices.
